transcription.text: In this New York apartment building overlooking Central Park in the lights of the city, a well-known caterer is preparing an after-show party for the cast of Louis the Louse. Right, Sid? They'll all be here, the whole cast, thirsty and hungry like all actors after a show. So we have plenty of food ready, beer, and lots of calling red-cap ale on ice. You know, red-cap's the favorite ale at most parties I cater. Why? Because people who really know good ale go for the heartier flavor of red-cap. It's got what I call heft, a taste you remember. And here, you see that rich, deep amber color? Let's you know that red-cap is special, hearty enough to satisfy the appetites of the show crowd. Yes, red-cap's got heft. It's all ale, and it does the greatest disappearing act in show business. Enjoy calling red-cap ale yourself. It's got heft. It does the greatest disappearing act in show business.
